scenario.context: Station 8 of the daily run: wrap up security, availability, and green IT.
scenario.mission: You assess security measures, power supply, and a sustainable wrap-up for network operations.

station.profile: Backup, availability, UPS, and green IT in the client network

customerRequest.subject: Station 08: prepare a secure and sustainable operational approval
customerRequest.body: Please assess the security baseline, power supply, and green IT measures for client network operations. We need a short wrap-up note with availability and sustainability context.

questions.title: Secure operational approval
questions.prompt: Which points belong in a secure operational approval? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Current updates and protection measures are active
options.rationale: This creates a solid security baseline.

options.text: Access rights are traceable and limited to what is necessary
options.rationale: This keeps the authorization concept intact.

options.text: Relevant data is secured by backup or central synchronization
One shared admin password for everyone is sufficient
options.rationale: That contradicts a secure roles and permissions concept.

options.text: Open network access without authentication makes operations easier
options.rationale: That would be a clear security risk.

feedback.correct: Correct: You secure protection, rights, and data baseline together.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: think more tightly about protection measures, rights, and data backup.

questions.title: Wrap-up for secure network operations
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for a clean final approval.
items: Create wrap-up note and monitoring hint
Check backup or central data protection
Check protection measures and rights baseline
Check power supply and outage precautions
correctOrder: Check protection measures and rights baseline
Check backup or central data protection
Check power supply and outage precautions
Create wrap-up note and monitoring hint

feedback.correct: Correct: This keeps approval secure and traceable.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: first check protection, backup, and power supply, then record residual risks.

questions.title: Handling unstable power supply
questions.prompt: What is most sensible for a small network cabinet with unstable power supply?

options.text: Plan a UPS and document an orderly shutdown
options.rationale: This improves availability and reduces follow-up damage.

options.text: Ignore power outages as long as they are rare
options.rationale: Rare outages can still cause damage.
That does not solve the power supply issue.

feedback.correct: Correct: Supply reliability and orderly operations belong together.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: actively secure and document the power supply.

questions.title: Estimate energy demand
questions.prompt: A network cabinet needs an average of 420 watts. It runs 10 hours per workday over 20 workdays. What is the energy demand in kWh?

feedback.wrong: Convert watts to kW and multiply by time.

questions.title: Wrap-up note for operations
questions.prompt: Formulate in 4-6 sentences a wrap-up note with security status, availability context, and energy note.
questions.expectedOutline: Connect security status, operational stability, and a green IT note in a short wrap-up.

feedback.correct: Correct: The wrap-up note connects protection, operations, and sustainability well.
feedback.wrong: Please connect security status, availability context, and energy note more clearly.
